sections.paragraph: (prob. i.q. מִירָה “an enclosure,” “an encampment of Nomades,” from the root טוּר of the form יְקוּם), pr.n. Jetûr, a son of Ishmael, Gen 25:15 1Ch 1:31 and his descendants the Iturœans, dwelling beyond Jordan, near the foot of Hermon, and on the eastern shore of the sea of Galilee, 1Ch 5:19, 20 1Ch 5:20, the region which was afterwards the province of Ituræa (Luc. iii. 1; Relandi Palæstina, p. 106), at present the district of Jeiןr (جيدور, Burckhardt’s Travels in Syria, p. 447). More has been said on this subject by Ilgen on the book of Job, p. 93, 94, and Fr. Münter in Progr. de Rebus Ituræorum ad Luc. iii. 1; Hafniæ, 1824, 4to.
